I think one… place that, we haven't had that set up before, so I think we didn't, have, like, our guidance super tight around that, but, one limitation of our system is that, only one account on Maven can be linked to one user within Meta Business Suite.

And so, like, say you had a scenario where, say, you have access to… just making this up, you have access to 10 properties on Meta Business Suite, and then somebody else has access to 5 properties on Meta Business Suite.

If you both log in to the shared account, and then authenticate to Meta, so, like, the first person will link the shared account on Maven to the Meta account that they log into, and then say somebody comes in afterwards and says, oh, I want to authorize 5 more properties, right?
If they log into the shared account on Maven, and then they go over to Meta, and they authenticate while they're on the Maven shared account.
the authentication from the previous person who was logged into the shared account gets broken.
And so it's down to whoever most recently logged in and authenticated via the shared account on Maven.

And so… I think… I would say the guidance we would probably give in this circumstance is that Anybody who is going to be doing… anybody who has administrative privileges on specific Meta Business Suite accounts should have a unique account in the Maven system.
And they should only authenticate to Meta while they're logged into that… their individual, Maven account.
